name: Minamoto Yoshiie
description: Minamoto Yoshiie (1039-1106), also known as Hachimantarō, was a prominent Minamoto clan samurai of the late Heian period. He was the eldest son of Minamoto no Yoriyoshi and played a crucial role in the Former Nine Years' War and the Gosannen War. Yoshiie's military prowess and leadership earned him great respect and established him as a legendary figure. During the Former Nine Years' War, he fought alongside his father to quell the rebellion led by Abe no Sadato in Mutsu Province. His bravery and strategic thinking were instrumental in securing victory for the Minamoto clan. He is particularly renowned for his involvement in the Gosannen War, where he independently led a campaign to suppress Kiyohara no Iehira and Kiyohara no Narihira. Yoshiie's commitment to maintaining order and his unwavering dedication to the imperial court solidified his reputation as a loyal and capable warrior. Stories of his legendary strength, unwavering honor, and strategic genius have been passed down through generations, making him a symbol of samurai virtue and a celebrated figure in Japanese history and folklore. He is often depicted as an ideal warrior, embodying bushido principles. His campaigns helped solidify Minamoto influence in northern Japan and set the stage for future Minamoto dominance.
subtitle: The Warrior of Hachiman

keyMyths.title: The Bird's Flight
keyMyths.description: Yoshiie's keen observation of a flock of birds revealed the location of an enemy ambush. This story highlights his strategic mind and awareness.

keyMyths.title: The River Crossing
keyMyths.description: During the Gosannen War, Yoshiie's forces faced a raging river. His leadership and courage inspired his men to overcome the obstacle and continue the pursuit.

keyMyths.title: The Arrow's Song
keyMyths.description: It is said that Yoshiie could judge the spirit of a warrior by the sound of their bow being drawn. This showcases his deep understanding of the warrior's path.

sources.author: Kitabatake Chikafusa
sources.description: A historical and political treatise that mentions Yoshiie's contributions.
